OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE

JCS Jonathan

Thank you for the ongoing hard work of your teams.

We discussed our overall plans this morning.
+ We would like to be ready to move to the next stage of our preparations
tomorrow mornina. and to reference this in whatever form of remarks the PM
makes tonight

Could | please see asap the document we would be publishing, even ffi in draft.
form, and the overall framing? Ideally this would come with

we are expecting around midday, but let me know when if you don’t have this
to hand. We aaree as discussed vesterday that it should be focused on ***

* On Ni, thank you for the additional advice. Please can we task the EUS and
NIO to ensure we have contingency plans in place incase ***

becomes overwhelmed by demand? Jes
@lsabel Abbs tba
* Stakeholder engagement. This is in a better place and we would welcome
sight of any supportive quotes that issue from business immediately over the
next 24 hrs on comms and policy channels (e.g. included in the daily update)
+ Comms. As referenced above if the announcement is made around 9pm, the
PM will likely make a statement. We know SoS DBT is planning a parly
statement. We are planning some business engagement tomorrow and our biz
teams are in touch.

Ailsa
Ailsa Terry CMG| Private Secretary for Foreign Affairs] Prime Minister’ s Office
| 10 Downing Street, London, SW1A 2AA|T: _ Personal

Subject: RE: US tariffs - PM readout and commission
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE
Dear Ailsa, 
,
Thank you for this readout and the letter from DBT SoS. The Chancellor agrees with the plan 
to work up a Call for Evidence on 
 to be launched 3rd of April. HMT officials
will pick up with DBT and CO counterparts on the next steps you have set out. As per 
discussions at Cabinet and elsewhere, CX would like this work to include consideration of the 
following:
•
 the Chancellor asks that, in addition to the advice requested by Number
10:
 Please could the final document and future policy
advice/handling plans be jointly agreed with HMT before being submitted to the PM.
I am also copying our plans for business engagement over the next couple of days below, 
which the team is discussing in more detail with 
.
Best, 
JCS
JCS

www.gov.uk/hm-treasury | Twitter: @hmtreasury
Bilateral meetings:
• Oracle (CX)
• BlackRock (CX)
• CVC Capital Partners (CX)
• CBI (EST)
• Astra Zeneca (FST)
• Barclays (CX and EST)
• Quantexa (CX)
• BAE Systems (MfI)
• AirBus (MfI)
• Melrose Industries (MfI)
Set pieces
• US Chamber of Commerce roundtable (FST)
• AIM roundtable (EST and Varun Chandra)
• Trade Strategy roundtable - Babcock, Shell, Starling Bank, Aston Martin and KPMG.
(CX and DBT SoS)
• Business breakfast – expected to be attended by M&G, Apollo, Carlyle Group, CPP,
Bloomberg, Vanguard and Nationwide (EST)
• Financial services IS deep dive – expected to be attended by HSBC, Clearbank,
Allica Bank, Atom Bank, Octopus Investments etc (EST)
• Deep dives on creative industries, digital and tech and clean energy sectors (SCS)
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE

All
The Prime Minister read the letter from SoS DBT and also from the AG overnight, 
ahead of the US discussion at Cabinet during which he also gave a number of steers.
The Prime Minister instructed that we remain focused on securing a good deal for the 
UK with the US which would be the best outcome. He also agreed the 
recommendations in the SoS DBT letter and has asked us to ensure we are able to 
announce we are moving to the next stage of
